title: Review Whether Tote Exchange Configuration Uses Count, Cube, Or Cube And Count Threshold Basis
summary: Use the Tote Exchange Settings Aveva API page to identify which documented Method option is configured for tote exchange evaluation. The source supports reading the Method field and confirming that the screen includes count-based and cube-based threshold categories, but it does not define calculation logic beyond the listed options.
when_to_use: Use this runbook when you need to verify from the Tote Exchange Settings interface whether tote exchange is configured to use Count, Cube, or Cube and Count as the documented method basis.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to change Tote Exchange Settings.
Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented calculation logic for the Cube and Count option.
Do not use this runbook to determine save, edit, or update actions because the source does not provide them.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Tote Exchange Settings interface, identify the Method field, compare the displayed value to the documented options, and record the configured threshold basis.

safety_notes: This source-supported procedure is a view/reference activity only.
Do not infer exact calculation behavior for the combined method because the source only states that it allows a mixture of both.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Tote Exchange Settings interface

access_required: Access to the Tote Exchange Settings interface
system_preconditions: The Tote Exchange Settings interface must be available for viewing.
The Method field must be visible on the Tote Exchange Settings screen.

visual_references.description: Tote Exchange Settings page showing threshold fields and Method options Count, Cube, and Cube and Count.

steps.title: Open or view the Tote Exchange Settings interface
steps.purpose: Access the source-supported screen where the Method field and threshold categories are shown.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Tote Exchange Settings interface on the Aveva API page.
steps.expected_result: The Tote Exchange Settings screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The Tote Exchange Settings interface is displayed and the settings area can be reviewed.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote Exchange Settings interface cannot be accessed or the relevant settings page is not visible.

actions.label: View Tote Exchange Settings
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Tote Exchange Settings interface.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Tote Exchange Settings page with the Method field and threshold entries visible.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Tote Exchange Settings interface is not available for viewing.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Tote Exchange Settings interface enables users to view and manage parameters related to the tote exchange process.

steps.title: Locate the Method field
steps.purpose: Find the specific field that identifies the tote exchange basis configured on the screen.
steps.instruction: Locate the Method field on the Tote Exchange Settings screen.
steps.expected_result: The Method field is identified on the interface.
steps.healthy_condition: A Method field is visible and readable on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The Method field is missing, obscured, or unreadable.

actions.label: Find Method field
actions.exact_action: Locate the Method field on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Method field area listing the available method choices.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Method field is missing.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR lists Method with options Count, Cube, Cube and Count.

steps.title: Read the configured Method option
steps.purpose: Determine which documented threshold basis is currently shown on the screen.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed Method value and compare it to the documented choices: Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
steps.expected_result: The configured method basis is identified as one of the documented options.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed Method value matches one of the documented options: Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed Method value does not match the documented options or cannot be determined.

actions.label: Read Method value
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed method option and compare it to the documented choices: Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected Method value and the listed options Count, Cube, and Cube and Count.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Method field shows a value not documented in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The software has the ability to do it based off counts. And so the method is either counts cube or ... a mixture of both.
OCR lists Method with options Count, Cube, Cube and Count.

steps.title: Confirm the related threshold categories on the screen
steps.purpose: Verify that the interface includes the count-based and cube-based threshold entries referenced by the source.
steps.instruction: Use the surrounding threshold fields to confirm that the screen includes count-based and cube-based threshold entries referenced by the source.
steps.expected_result: The screen shows threshold categories for cube and count, including pickup, priority, and close entries.
steps.healthy_condition: Cube Threshold Pickup, Cube Threshold Priority, Cube Threshold Close, Count Threshold Pickup, Count Threshold Priority, and Count Threshold Close are visible or otherwise confirmed from the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The expected cube-based or count-based threshold categories are not visible or do not align with the documented screen content.

actions.label: Inspect threshold categories
actions.exact_action: Use the surrounding threshold fields to confirm that the screen includes count-based and cube-based threshold entries referenced by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Threshold fields labeled for cube and count, including pickup, priority, and close.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the threshold categories shown on the screen do not include the documented count-based and cube-based entries.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You have a queue, you have a pick up, a priority and a close... those are your thresholds.
Screen lists Cube Threshold Pickup, Cube Threshold Priority, Cube Threshold Close, Count Threshold Pickup, Count Threshold Priority, and Count Threshold Close.

steps.title: Record the configured threshold basis
steps.purpose: Capture the observed Method setting without extending beyond what the source documents.
steps.instruction: Record which threshold basis is configured without inferring any undocumented software behavior beyond the listed options.
steps.expected_result: The configured basis is recorded as Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded result matches the displayed Method field and stays within the documented options.
steps.failure_condition: The result cannot be recorded confidently from the screen or requires unsupported interpretation.

actions.label: Document configured basis
actions.exact_action: Record which threshold basis is configured without inferring any undocumented software behavior beyond the listed options.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Method field value used as the basis for the recorded result.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Method field is missing or shows a value not documented in the source.
Stop and avoid further interpretation if determining the result would require inferring exact combined-method calculation logic.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The software has the ability to do it based off counts. And so the method is either counts cube or ... a mixture of both.
OCR lists Method with options Count, Cube, Cube and Count.

success_criteria: The Tote Exchange Settings interface is viewed successfully.
The Method field is located and read.
The configured method is identified as Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
The surrounding threshold categories for cube-based and count-based entries are confirmed on the screen.
The result is recorded without inferring undocumented behavior.
healthy_conditions: The Tote Exchange Settings page is visible.
The Method field is present and readable.
The Method value matches one of the documented options: Count, Cube, or Cube and Count.
The screen includes documented threshold categories for cube and count.
failure_conditions: The Tote Exchange Settings interface cannot be accessed for viewing.
The Method field is missing, unreadable, or not visible.
The Method field shows a value not documented in the source.
The threshold categories on the screen do not align with the documented cube and count threshold entries.
Determining the result would require unsupported inference about software logic.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the Method field is missing or shows a value not documented in the source.
Escalate if the Tote Exchange Settings screen does not show the documented threshold categories.
Do not infer exact calculation logic for the combined method because the source only states that it allows a mixture of both.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote Exchange Settings page with Method field and threshold categories.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The software has the ability to do it based off counts. And so the method is either counts cube or ... a mixture of both.
OCR lists Method with options Count, Cube, Cube and Count.

missing_details: The source does not provide navigation steps to reach the Tote Exchange Settings page.
The source does not provide edit, save, or update actions for the Method field.
The source does not define exact calculation logic for the Cube and Count method.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this review activity.
The source does not specify production-stop or LOTO requirements.
